name: standup
tagline: Facilitates a read-only multi-agent 'standup' chat across git worktrees/branches/PRs to produce one consolidation plan.
verdict: Solid, low-risk skill: it stays explicitly read-only, never merges or commits itself, gates real git work behind a separate /do skill, and gives the human concrete decisions via AskUserQuestion. Only minor gaps: no negative-trigger guidance, and 'Edit' is declared in allowed-tools but never used in the described read-only flow. Bundled standup.mjs/agent-brie

highlights.summary: Declares 'Edit' in allowed-tools (line 8) but the described flow is explicitly read-only and never shows Edit being invoked — minor mismatch between declared capability and stated behavior.
No explicit negative-trigger guidance (when not to use it) beyond the implicit 'zero or one candidate isn't a standup' guard (line 46).
Bash, Task, AskUserQuestion, and Read are all clearly exercised; Edit is declared but has no corresponding use in the described flow (see S11).
